fields.equipment: Rapier, Leather Armour, Rope, Waterskin, Bedroll, Rations x5, Hunting Trap, Net, Cook's Utensils.
fields.treasureNotes: 4 daggers, leather armour, 15 caltrops, 3 beads of nourishment.

feats.name: Bardic Inspiration (Class)
feats.details: As a Bonus Action, you can inspire another creature within 60 ft. that can see or hear you. That creature gains one of your Bardic Inspiration dice (1d6). Once within the next hour, when the creature fails a D20 Test, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the total, potentially turning the failure into a success.

feats.name: Reactive (Background)
feats.details: Your survival instincts sharpen your reflexes- add PB to Initiative (+2 at Level 1).

feats.name: Poison Resilience (Species)
feats.details: Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the poisoned condition, plus resistance to poison damage.

feats.name: Spider Sense (Species)
feats.details: Extra pair of eyes grants proficiency in Perception.

feats.name: Spider Climb (Species)
feats.details: Climb speed equal to walking speed. At level 3 you can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and ceilings while keeping your hands free.

feats.name: Arachnid Bite (Species)
feats.details: Must grapple target first, then use bonus action for a poisonous bite,. Damage is 1d6+CON modifier. Target risks being poisoned for 1 minute unless they pass a Constitution save (DC = 8 + PB + CON modifier).

feats.name: Arachnid Casting (Species)
feats.details:
Level 1: Mending Cantrip
Level 3: Ray of Sickness spell
Level 5: Web spell

Spellcasting ability is Charisma.

feats.name: Everything is Edible (Background)
feats.details: Once per long rest, spend 1 hour foraging to craft a number of rations equal to your PB (2 at Level 1). When a creature eats one during a short rest they gain a d4 they can add to one saving throw after rolling but before knowing the outcome. Lasts until long rest. Rations spoil after 24 hours.

spells.name: Message
spells.effect: You point toward a creature within range and whisper a message. The target (and only the target) hears the message and can reply in a whisper that only you can hear.
spells.details: You point toward a creature within range and whisper a message. The target (and only the target) hears the message and can reply in a whisper that only you can hear. You can cast this spell through solid objects if you are familiar with the target and know it is beyond the barrier. Magical silence; 1 foot of stone, metal, or wood; or a thin sheet of lead blocks the spell.

spells.name: Vicious Mockery
spells.effect: You unleash a string of insults laced with subtle enchantments at one creature you can see or hear within range.
spells.details:
You unleash a string of insults laced with subtle enchantments at one creature you can see or hear within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take 1d6 Psychic damage and have Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

spells.name: Bane
spells.effect: Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within range must each make a Charisma saving throw.
spells.details:
Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within range must each make a Charisma saving throw. Whenever a target that fails this save makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target must subtract 1d4 from the attack roll or save.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.
spells.componentsText: V, S, M (a drop of blood)

spells.name: Healing Word
spells.effect: A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
spells.details:
A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Siren's Call
spells.effect: A warning cry blasts creatures in a cone for 2d8 Thunder damage and pushes them away on a failed Constitution save.
spells.details:
Through the echoes, your call for help shall be heard. A defensive spell created by the magic researchers of the Aeternia kingdom. As you cry for help, you forcefully push away those who mean you harm. However, be mindful, as sound transfers differently under water.

On Land. Each creature within a 15-foot cone makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take 2d8 Thunder damage and are pushed away from you by 5 feet. On a successful save, they take half the damage and are not pushed. Your call travels over 100 feet on land.

Under Water. Each creature within a 20-foot cone makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take 2d8 Thunder damage and are pushed away from you by 15 feet. On a successful save, they take half the damage and are not pushed. Your call travels over 300 feet through water.

Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

spells.name: Thunderwave
spells.effect: You unleash a wave of thunderous energy. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw.
spells.details:
You unleash a wave of thunderous energy. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. In addition, unsecured objects that are entirely within the Cube are pushed 10 feet away from you, and a thunderous boom is audible within 300 feet.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

inventoryItems.name: Beads of Nourishment
inventoryItems.details:
Magic Item · Common

Wondrous Item, Common

This flavorless, gelatinous bead dissolves on your tongue and provides as much nourishment as 1 day of Rations.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Bedroll
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 7 lb.

A Bedroll sleeps one Small or Medium creature. While in a Bedroll, you automatically succeed on saving throws against extreme cold (see “Gameplay Toolbox”).

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Caltrops
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 2 lb.

As a Utilize action, you can spread Caltrops from their bag to cover a 5-footsquare area within 5 feet of yourself. A creature that enters this area for the first time on a turn must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 1 Piercing damage and have its Speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. It takes 10 minutes to recover the Caltrops.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Cook's Utensils
inventoryItems.details:
Tool

Ability: Wisdom Weight: 8 lb. Utilize: Improve food’s flavor (DC 10), or detect spoiled or poisoned food (DC 15) Craft: Rations

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Daggers
inventoryItems.details:
Weapon · Weight: 1 lb.

1d4 Piercing; Finesse, Light, Thrown (Range 20/60); Nick

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Hunting Trap
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 25 lb.

As a Utilize action, you can set a Hunting Trap, which is a sawtooth steel ring that snaps shut when a creature steps on a pressure plate in the center. 98 The trap is affixed by a heavy chain to an immobile object, such as a tree or a spike driven into the ground. A creature that steps on the plate must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 1d4 Piercing damage and have its Speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. Thereafter, until the creature breaks free of the trap, its movement is limited by the length of the chain (typically 3 feet). A creature can use its action to make a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Each failed check deals 1 Piercing damage to the trapped creature.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Leather Armour
inventoryItems.details:
Armor · Weight: 10 lb.

11 + Dex modifier

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Net
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 3 lb.

When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a Net. Target a creature you can see within 15 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or have the Restrained condition until it escapes. The target succeeds automatically if it is Huge or larger. To escape, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 10 Strength (Athletics) check, freeing the Restrained creature on a success. Destroying the Net (AC 10; 5 HP; Immunity to Bludgeoning, Poison, and Psychic damage) also frees the target, ending the effect.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Rapier
inventoryItems.details:
Weapon · Weight: 2 lb.

1d8 Piercing; Finesse; Vex

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Rations
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 2 lb.

Rations consist of travelready food, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See “Malnutrition” in “Rules Glossary” for the risks of not eating.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Rope
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 5 lb.

As a Utilize action, you can tie a knot with Rope if you succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. The Rope can be burst with a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check. You can bind an unwilling creature with the Rope only if the creature has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition. If the creature’s legs are bound, the creature has the Restrained condition until it escapes. Escaping the Rope requires the creature to make a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryItems.name: Waterskin
inventoryItems.details:
Adventuring Gear · Weight: 5 lb. (full)

A Waterskin holds up to 4 pints. If you don’t drink sufficient water, you risk dehydration (see “Rules Glossary”). A mount can help you move more quickly through the wilderness, but its primary purpose is to carry gear that would otherwise slow you down. The Mounts and Other Animals table shows each animal’s carrying capacity. See “Monsters” for the animals’ stat blocks. An animal pulling a carriage, cart, chariot, sled, or wagon can move weight up to five times its base carrying capacity, including the weight of the vehicle. If multiple animals pull the same vehicle, add their carrying capacities together. Barding is armor designed for a mount. Any type of armor on the Armor table in “Equipment” can be purchased as barding. The cost is four times the normal cost, and it weighs twice as much. A saddle comes with a bit, a bridle, reins, and any other equipment needed to use the saddle. A Military Saddle gives Advantage on any ability check you make to remain mounted. An Exotic Saddle is required for riding an aquatic or a flying mount. Mounts and Other Animals Item Carrying Capacity Cost Camel 450 lb. 50 GP Elephant 1,320 lb. 200 GP Horse, Draft 540 lb. 50 GP Horse, Riding 480 lb. 75 GP Mastiff 195 lb. 25 GP Mule 420 lb. 8 GP Pony 225 lb. 30 GP Warhorse 540 lb. 400 GP Item Weight Cost Carriage 600 lb. 100 GP Cart 200 lb. 15 GP Chariot 100 lb. 250 GP Feed per day 10 lb. 5 CP Saddle Exotic 40 lb. 60 GP Military 30 lb. 20 GP Riding 25 lb. 10 GP Sled 300 lb. 20 GP Stabling per day — 5 SP Wagon 400 lb. 35 GP The Airborne and Waterborne Vehicles table provides statistics for various types of large vehicles. The following notes apply. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can’t move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can’t be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

Source: SRD 5.2.1

inventoryEquipment.details:
